front: Define a projection in linear algebra
back: A projection is a linear transformation P: V→V that maps vectors onto a subspace such that applying it twice yields the same result, i.e., P² = P.

front: What is the condition for a matrix to be a projection matrix?
back: A matrix P is a projection matrix if and only if P² = P (idempotent) and P = Pᵀ (symmetric) for orthogonal projections.

front: Define orthogonal projection onto a subspace
back: The orthogonal projection of vector v onto subspace W is the closest point in W to v, where the difference v - proj_W(v) is orthogonal to W.

front: Formula for projecting vector v onto subspace spanned by vector u
What is the projection matrix onto column space of matrix A?
back: P = A(AᵀA)⁻¹Aᵀ, applicable when A has full column rank.
An oblique projection is a projection where the projection direction is not perpendicular to the subspace; the projection matrix satisfies P² = P but is not symmetric.

front: What is the null space of a projection matrix?
back: The null space of P consists of vectors that are mapped to zero, i.e., vectors orthogonal to the column space for orthogonal projections.

front: Relationship between I - P and projection matrix
back: I - P is also a projection matrix that projects onto the orthogonal complement (null space of Pᵀ).

front: What is the rank of a projection matrix?
back: The rank of projection matrix P equals the dimension of the subspace onto which it projects (the trace of P).
The least squares solution to Ax = b minimizes ||Ax - b||², given by x = (AᵀA)⁻¹Aᵀb when A has full rank.

front: What is the pseudoinverse of A?
back: A⁺ = (AᵀA)⁻¹Aᵀ for matrices with full column rank; used to find least squares solution as x = A⁺b.
Projection drops a perpendicular from a vector to a subspace, finding the closest point in that subspace.
1) P² = P (idempotent), 2) P = Pᵀ (symmetric), 3) eigenvalues are 0 or 1, 4) P is positive semi-definite.

front: Application of projections in computer graphics
back: Projections are used for 3D to 2D transformations, perspective projections, and rendering scenes onto 2D screens.

front: Application of projections in machine learning
back: Projections are used in PCA (Principal Component Analysis) to project data onto principal components, reducing dimensionality.
